claim: EVA vowel characters (a,o,e,i), currently stripped by the consonant-skeleton pipeline, encode real phonetic distinctions in Syriac vowels: EVA 'e'→ī, EVA 'o'→o/u, EVA 'a'→a/ā. Disambiguation layer resolves 7,007 tokens (20% of corpus) at >=70% confidence.
evidence_for: Skeleton 'kl' (1,059 tokens) splits into three populations via vowel pattern
130 tokens of _o_a_ pattern (e.g. 'okal') read as kuhla ('collyrium') on pharma pages
'chedy' vs 'chody' (same skeleton kdy) show sectional difference scores 78-110
evidence_against: Vowel-pattern assignment may be circular: is kuhla concentrated on pharma pages because of the encoding rule, or fit to that distribution?
Independent test requires separate vowel-disambiguation implementation
test: Independent: partition 'chedy' vs 'chody' tokens by section; chi-square test for non-random distribution
test_threshold: chi-square p<0.001: confirmed vowels carry info. p>0.05: refuted.
tests_remaining: Independent: partition 'chedy' vs 'chody' tokens by section; chi-square test for non-random distribution
reasoning: Partially testable now; full test requires vowel-layer implementation.
source: Brady Defibaugh (April 2026), A Syriac Pharmaceutical Dispensatory Encoded in the Voynich Manuscript (DANI pipeline v31)
